share.share_text: Creator Andrew Davenport spent years developing the 2007 childrenâs series In the Night Garden with a massive production budget, utilizing a highly unique technical approach that blended real-life actors in elaborate costumed puppetry with advanced digital tracking. To capture the ethereal, Dreamscape atmosphere of the forest environment, the production team filmed entirely on location within a real, secluded woodland in Warwickshire, constructing custom pathways and hidden camera rigs to make the characters appear naturally scaled alongside gigantic trees. Voice actor Derek Jacobi provided the calm, hypnotic narration for the entire series, meticulously pacing his delivery to serve as a soothing, low-energy auditory guide designed to prepare preschool children for sleep. For character development, Davenport structured the distinct repetitive languages and signature songs for Igglepiggle, Upsy Daisy, and Makka Pakka based on modern linguistic research into early childhood sound development and syllables. This ambitious project represented a major creative milestone for the BBC, relying on an enormous, custom-built outdoor set and specialized visual effects to create a seamless, comforting world that became a global television phenomenon.
